records.ety: [OE. trough, trogh, AS. trog, troh; akin to D., G., & Icel. trog, Sw. trg, Dan. trug; probably originally meaning, made of wood, and akin to E. tree. √63 & 241. See Tree, and cf. Trug.]

defs: A long, hollow vessel, generally for holding water or other liquid, especially one formed by excavating a log longitudinally on one side; a long tray; also, a wooden channel for conveying water, as to a mill wheel.
Any channel, receptacle, or depression, of a long and narrow shape; as, trough between two ridges, etc.
The transverse section of a cyclonic area where the barometric pressure, neither rising nor falling, has reached its lowest point.
